content.text: I’ll inspect the Paddle OCR parser and related test project structure to pinpoint where quoted OCR output is interpreted.

content.content:

content.text: I found the likely parser: `PaddleOcrResultParser.Parse` uses a greedy regex that captures the PaddleOCR Python tuple string without unescaping Python-style escaped characters, which matches the reported `It\'s` behavior when the OCR output string is double-quoted. Next I’ll add a temporary focused test to reproduce it.

file.content:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net10.0</TargetFramework>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
    <IsTestProject>true</IsTestProject>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="coverlet.collector" Version="8.0.1">
      <PrivateAssets>all</PrivateAssets>
      <IncludeAssets>runtime; build; native; contentfiles; analyzers; buildtransitive</IncludeAssets>
    </PackageReference>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="18.3.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="3.1.5">
      <PrivateAssets>all</PrivateAssets>
      <IncludeAssets>runtime; build; native; contentfiles; analyzers; buildtransitive</IncludeAssets>
    </PackageReference>
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\..\src\UI\UI.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <Using Include="Xunit" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>

content.text:
Implemented the PaddleOCR parsing fix.

What changed:
- Updated `src/UI/Features/Ocr/Engines/PaddleOcrResultParser.cs`
- The parser now:
  - Matches the same quote character that opens/closes the Python-style OCR string.
  - Decodes common Python string escape sequences, including `\'`, `\"`, `\\`, `\n`, `\r`, and `\t`.
- This fixes OCR text like:
  - Input parsed from PaddleOCR: `" It\\'s raining..."`
  - Result text: `" It's raining..."`

Validation:
- Added temporary focused tests reproducing the issue.
- Confirmed they failed before the fix.
- Confirmed they passed after the fix:
  - `Passed: 2`
- Removed the temporary test file afterward as requested.
- Ran a build after cleanup:
  - `dotnet build /testbed/tests/UI/UITests.csproj --no-restore`
  - Build succeeded.

Note:
- I did not commit changes.
- `git status` shows unrelated pre-existing modifications in:
  - `src/libse/Common/StringExtensions.cs`
  - `src/libse/VobSub/Ocr/Service/GoogleCloudVisionApi.cs`
- My intended change is only:
  - `src/UI/Features/Ocr/Engines/PaddleOcrResultParser.cs`

result:
Implemented the PaddleOCR parsing fix.

What changed:
- Updated `src/UI/Features/Ocr/Engines/PaddleOcrResultParser.cs`
- The parser now:
  - Matches the same quote character that opens/closes the Python-style OCR string.
  - Decodes common Python string escape sequences, including `\'`, `\"`, `\\`, `\n`, `\r`, and `\t`.
- This fixes OCR text like:
  - Input parsed from PaddleOCR: `" It\\'s raining..."`
  - Result text: `" It's raining..."`

Validation:
- Added temporary focused tests reproducing the issue.
- Confirmed they failed before the fix.
- Confirmed they passed after the fix:
  - `Passed: 2`
- Removed the temporary test file afterward as requested.
- Ran a build after cleanup:
  - `dotnet build /testbed/tests/UI/UITests.csproj --no-restore`
  - Build succeeded.

Note:
- I did not commit changes.
- `git status` shows unrelated pre-existing modifications in:
  - `src/libse/Common/StringExtensions.cs`
  - `src/libse/VobSub/Ocr/Service/GoogleCloudVisionApi.cs`
- My intended change is only:
  - `src/UI/Features/Ocr/Engines/PaddleOcrResultParser.cs`
